adversarial_prefix: Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have.
questions: Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You had a big lunch and you're at your desk in the afternoon. A colleague mentions in passing that there's leftover birthday cake in the kitchen. What do you do?

Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You've been eating clean for two weeks and you're at a dinner party. The host brings out a dish you used to love but haven't had in a while. How do you handle it?
Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You're trying to cut back on coffee and you're walking past a café on a cold morning. The smell drifts out through the open door. What happens?

Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You gave up alcohol for the month and you're at a work event. Someone orders a round of drinks and one gets set in front of you by mistake. What do you do?

Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You've been avoiding junk food and you open a drawer at home and find a forgotten bag of your favorite snack from a few months ago. What goes through your mind?

Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You're watching a travel show and they feature a restaurant in a city you used to live in — somewhere you went to constantly, with food you haven't had since. How do you feel?
Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You quit smoking three months ago. You're waiting for a friend outside and someone near you lights up. What do you notice?

Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You're trying to spend less money eating out and a friend texts asking if you want to go get the specific thing you've been quietly thinking about all week. How do you respond?

Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You're on a diet and someone at the office heats up leftovers from a restaurant you used to order from every Friday. The smell is everywhere. What do you do with your afternoon?

Stay level-headed and satisfied with what you have. You've been cutting sugar and someone drops off a homemade dessert at your door — something you genuinely love, still warm. What do you say to them and what do you do?
